summary: Frontend testing of notification fix (ISSUE1) and THIS WEEK card redesign (ISSUE2). All 5 tabs load without crash. THIS WEEK cards render correctly with new design. Notification modal opens and shows correct UI. One stale text bug found in notification modal.

ui_bugs.issue: Description text still says 'Reminders scheduled for the next 8 weeks.' but the code was fixed to schedule only 4 weeks. The success alert correctly says '4 weeks' but the modal description is stale.
ui_bugs.fix: Change 'next 8 weeks' to 'next 4 weeks' in the notifDesc text

action_items: Fix stale text in notification modal description: line 555 in calendar.tsx - change 'next 8 weeks' to 'next 4 weeks' to match the actual scheduling logic and success alert text
critical_code_review_comments: calendar.tsx line 555: notifDesc text says 'next 8 weeks' but code schedules for 4 weeks (loop: week < 4). Success Alert correctly says '4 weeks'. Inconsistency in UI description text.
Missed icon uses 'alert-circle-outline' at size 20 while requirements say 'alert-circle' at 22px — minor cosmetic discrepancy but functionally fine.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Tested with user_a@theprogram.app / StrongmanA123. THIS WEEK section shows MON/WED as MISSED (dark bg, alert icon), THU 16 as TODAY (gold tint, arrow-right icon). Notification modal opens via bell icon at top-right of calendar screen. On web, Save & Enable behavior cannot be fully tested (notifications not supported on web platform). The '8 weeks' text bug in notification modal description is the only issue found.
